commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds message-tool result hashing that drops volatile delivery-id fields from `details` and adds loop-detection regression tests.

PR surface: Source +30, Tests +61. Total +91 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: repeated `message` sends whose result text is JSON-stringified details with changing `messageId` will still produce changing `resultHash` values under this patch; I did not run a live Feishu loop in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - The blocking defect is concrete and mechanically repairable in the same hashing function and regression tests without a product or config decision.

target.title: fix(agents): block message-tool spam loops defeated by volatile message ids
target.body:
## Summary

**Problem:** `loopDetection` detects repeated `message.send` calls (`ping_pong`, warning level) but can never escalate to a block. A model stuck in a tool-call loop keeps sending duplicate messages — 30+ duplicates reported — until `runRetries.max` (default 160) is exhausted, and the user only sees a flood of identical messages.

**Root cause:** In `tool-loop-detection.ts`, `hashToolOutcome` hashes the full result (`details` + `text`) for the `message` tool. Every successful send returns a fresh `messageId`, so the `resultHash` differs on every call. All three critical-block paths — the global circuit breaker, `ping_pong` critical, and generic no-progress — require consecutive **identical** `resultHash` values (`getNoProgressStreak` / `noProgressEvidence`), which is structurally impossible for a tool whose result carries a unique id. Detection fires only at warning level because it also keys on `argsHash`, which is stable.

**Fix:** Add a stable-hash case for the `message` tool that strips volatile delivery-id keys (`messageId`, `message_id`, `messageIds`, `id`, `receipt`, `ts`, `timestamp`) before hashing, preserving the stable success/target/channel/text facts. This mirrors the existing per-tool stable-hash cases for `exec` and `process` poll/log, and applies at the shared hashing layer so it covers direct, gateway, and plugin-handled message results. Repeated identical-args sends now produce a stable `resultHash`, so the no-progress and critical paths fire and the loop is blocked.

**Why this is safe (no false positives):** no-progress requires BOTH `argsHash` and `resultHash` to repeat. The args hash already carries the message content and target, so two genuinely different messages have different `argsHash` and are never flagged — dropping the volatile id only restores detection for true same-args resends.

**What changed:**
- `message`-tool result hashing in `tool-loop-detection.ts` now ignores volatile delivery ids.

**What did NOT change:**
- Generic / exec / process / poll detector semantics are untouched.
- No detector thresholds, retry caps, or limits were raised.
- No config, plugin SDK, or dependency surface.

## Reproduction

1. Configure an agent with a model prone to tool-call loops on a channel that returns a per-send message id (e.g. Feishu).
2. The model loops on `message.send` with identical arguments.
3. Before this change: `ping_pong` fires at warning level but never reaches critical/block; the user receives 30+ duplicate messages.

Deterministic source reproduction (added as a regression test): record repeated `message` sends with identical args but a changing `messageId` in the result, then call `detectToolCallLoop` — before the fix it can never reach a critical branch.

**Behavior addressed (message-tool loops can now be blocked, not just warned)**: repeated identical `message.send` escalates to a critical block instead of spamming until run-retry exhaustion.

**Real environment tested (deterministic loop-detection unit tests against the real module; no live channel run)**: verified via `tool-loop-detection.test.ts`, plus core and scoped test type checks and format checks. A live multi-channel gateway loop was not run.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch (repo-root commands)**:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json` (core production types)
- scoped `tsgo` over the changed production + test files (test types)
- `oxfmt --check` on the changed files

**Evidence after fix (46/46 unit tests pass; types and format clean)**: all loop-detection tests pass, including three new cases — stable hash across volatile ids, escalation to critical despite per-call ids, and no false-positive on distinct messages; `tsgo` core + scoped test types exit 0; `oxfmt --check` clean.

**Observed result after fix (identical-args send loops reach critical/block)**: the new escalation test confirms `detectToolCallLoop` reports `stuck` at `critical` for repeated identical sends, while distinct-content sends remain unflagged.

**What was not tested (live channel/gateway runaway loop)**: a real Feishu/gateway loop session was not reproduced live; the proof is the deterministic module-level reproduction the issue itself describes.

## Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk:** over-stripping result fields could collide distinct outcomes. **Mitigation:** only a small set of volatile delivery-id keys is removed; stable facts (status, target/chatId, action, text) and the `argsHash` gate remain, so distinct messages stay distinguishable (covered by a regression test).
- **Risk surface:** behavior change is scoped to the `message` tool only; other detectors and tools are untouched.

## Change Type
fix (behavior correctness)

## Scope
- Core only: `src/agents/tool-loop-detection.ts` plus its colocated test.
- Config surface unchanged. Plugin surface unchanged. Dependencies unchanged.

## Linked Issue
Fixes #89090
target.author: openperf

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
